name: Vaultwarden
tagline: Lightweight Bitwarden-compatible password manager.
description: Unofficial Bitwarden server written in Rust. Single binary, SQLite by default, drop-in compatible with the official Bitwarden clients. Ideal first install — small footprint, no external database required.
notes: Set an ADMIN_TOKEN env var to enable the /admin panel. Disable SIGNUPS_ALLOWED after creating your first user.
installPromptTemplate:
You are generating a Kubernetes manifest for the user's k3s cluster.

## App
Vaultwarden ({{instance}}) — Bitwarden-compatible password manager.

## Target
- Namespace: {{namespace}}
- Ingress hostname: {{hostname}}
- Node pin: {{nodeName}} (empty = no nodeSelector)
- Persistent storage: {{storage}}Gi
- Extra notes from user: {{notes}}

## Cluster conventions (MUST follow)
- Single Deployment + Service + Ingress + PersistentVolumeClaim, one YAML document each separated by `---`.
- Image: `vaultwarden/server:latest`, `imagePullPolicy: Always`.
- 1 replica (SQLite — no HA).
- Strategy: `Recreate` (PVC is RWO).
- Pod security: `runAsNonRoot: true`, `runAsUser: 65534`, `runAsGroup: 65534`, `fsGroup: 65534`. Container: `readOnlyRootFilesystem: false` (Vaultwarden writes to /data). Drop all caps.
- Env: `DOMAIN=https://{{hostname}}`, `SIGNUPS_ALLOWED=true` (user will lock down later), `WEBSOCKET_ENABLED=true`.
- PVC: `{{storage}}Gi`, default storageClass (leave unset).
- Container port 80 (HTTP) and 3012 (WebSocket).
- Service: ClusterIP, expose 80 and 3012.
- Ingress: `ingressClassName: traefik`. Annotations: `cert-manager.io/cluster-issuer: letsencrypt-prod`, `traefik.ingress.kubernetes.io/router.entrypoints: web,websecure`, `traefik.ingress.kubernetes.io/router.middlewares: default-redirect-https@kubernetescrd`. DO NOT set `router.tls: "true"`. TLS section: `hosts: [{{hostname}}]`, `secretName: {{instance}}-tls`.
- Resources: requests cpu 100m / mem 128Mi, limits cpu 500m / mem 512Mi.
- Labels: every resource gets `app.kubernetes.io/name: vaultwarden` and `app.kubernetes.io/instance: {{instance}}`.
- Readiness + liveness probe: `httpGet path=/alive port=80`, initialDelaySeconds 10.
- Image pull secret: `imagePullSecrets: [{name: ghrc}]` (cluster-wide convention even though Docker Hub is public).

## Output
Reply with ONE fenced ```yaml code block containing the full manifest. No prose outside the fence. No comments inside the YAML unless absolutely necessary. The YAML must apply cleanly with `kubectl apply -f -`.

You are generating a Kubernetes manifest for the user's k3s cluster.

## App
Supabase ({{instance}}) — open-source Firebase alternative. Self-hosted bundle: Postgres + GoTrue auth + PostgREST + Realtime + Storage + Kong API gateway + Studio.

## Target
- Namespace: {{namespace}}
- Public ingress hostname: {{hostname}} (Studio admin UI)
- Node pin: {{nodeName}} (empty = no nodeSelector)
- Postgres PVC size: {{storage}}Gi
- Extra notes from user: {{notes}}

## Cluster conventions (MUST follow)
- Generate placeholders for the secrets the user MUST fill in before applying: `JWT_SECRET` (32+ char random), `ANON_KEY` (JWT signed with JWT_SECRET, role=anon), `SERVICE_ROLE_KEY` (JWT signed with JWT_SECRET, role=service_role), `POSTGRES_PASSWORD`, `DASHBOARD_USERNAME`, `DASHBOARD_PASSWORD`. Surface these as `valueFrom: secretKeyRef` against a single Secret named `{{instance}}-secrets` — and include that Secret as the FIRST document with placeholder values clearly labeled `<FILL_ME_IN>`.
- All Deployments: 1 replica, `Recreate` strategy (PVC is RWO).
- Pod security: `runAsNonRoot: true`, drop all caps. Postgres uses `runAsUser: 999` (postgres uid in supabase/postgres image).
- Postgres: image `supabase/postgres:15.1.0.147`, PVC `{{storage}}Gi` mounted at `/var/lib/postgresql/data` (subPath `pgdata`).
- GoTrue: image `supabase/gotrue:v2.151.0`, env wired to Postgres via service DNS.
- PostgREST: image `postgrest/postgrest:v12.0.1`.
- Realtime: image `supabase/realtime:v2.25.66`.
- Storage: image `supabase/storage-api:v0.43.11`, PVC `5Gi` for file storage.
- Kong: image `kong:2.8.1`, declarative config via ConfigMap routing `/auth → gotrue:9999`, `/rest → postgrest:3000`, `/storage → storage:5000`, `/realtime → realtime:4000`.
- Studio: image `supabase/studio:20240101-ce42139`.
- Services: all ClusterIP. Only one Ingress, pointing to Studio on port 3000.
- Ingress: `ingressClassName: traefik`. Annotations: `cert-manager.io/cluster-issuer: letsencrypt-prod`, `traefik.ingress.kubernetes.io/router.entrypoints: web,websecure`, `traefik.ingress.kubernetes.io/router.middlewares: default-redirect-https@kubernetescrd`. DO NOT set `router.tls: "true"`. TLS section: `hosts: [{{hostname}}]`, `secretName: {{instance}}-tls`.
- Resources per service: requests 100–300m / 256–512Mi, limits 1 / 1Gi. Postgres gets more: 500m / 1Gi requests, 2 / 4Gi limits.
- Labels: every resource gets `app.kubernetes.io/name: supabase`, `app.kubernetes.io/instance: {{instance}}`, `app.kubernetes.io/component: <postgres|gotrue|postgrest|realtime|storage|kong|studio>`.
- Image pull secret: `imagePullSecrets: [{name: ghrc}]` on every pod spec.
- Readiness probes on each service appropriate to the image (postgres: `pg_isready`; HTTP services: `httpGet /health` or `/`).

## Output
Reply with ONE fenced ```yaml code block containing the full manifest as multiple `---`-separated documents. No prose outside the fence. The first document MUST be the placeholder `{{instance}}-secrets` Secret with `<FILL_ME_IN>` markers and a top comment instructing the user to fill before applying. The YAML must apply cleanly with `kubectl apply -f -` once secrets are filled.

## Target
- Namespace: {{namespace}}
- Ingress hostname: {{hostname}}
- Node pin: {{nodeName}}
- Postgres PVC: {{storage}}Gi; ClickHouse PVC: 30Gi; Kafka PVC: 10Gi
- Notes: {{notes}}

## Conventions
- Workloads (one Deployment each unless noted): `sentry-web` (image `getsentry/sentry:25.1.0`, runs `sentry run web`), `sentry-worker` (`sentry run worker`), `sentry-cron` (`sentry run cron`), `sentry-ingest` (`sentry run consumer ingest-events`), `snuba-api` (image `getsentry/snuba:25.1.0`), `snuba-consumer` (`snuba consumer --storage errors`), `relay` (image `getsentry/relay:25.1.0`). Plus dependency StatefulSets: `postgres:15-alpine`, `clickhouse/clickhouse-server:24.3-alpine`, `redis:7-alpine`, `bitnami/kafka:3.5`, `bitnami/zookeeper:3.9`, `memcached:1.6-alpine`.
- Secret `{{instance}}-secrets`: `SENTRY_SECRET_KEY` (50-char `<FILL_ME_IN>`), `POSTGRES_PASSWORD`, `CLICKHOUSE_PASSWORD`, initial admin email + password — all `<FILL_ME_IN>`.
- Env on every sentry-* pod: `SENTRY_REDIS_HOST=redis`, `SENTRY_POSTGRES_HOST=postgres`, `SENTRY_KAFKA_HOST=kafka:9092`, `SENTRY_DB_NAME=sentry`, `SENTRY_DB_USER=sentry`, `SENTRY_SECRET_KEY` from secretKeyRef.
- 1 replica per workload (HA is out of scope), `Recreate` strategy.
- Pod security: `runAsNonRoot: true`, drop all caps. Sentry image runs as user 999.
- Services: ClusterIP for everything; only `sentry-web` (port 9000) is ingressed.
- Ingress: `ingressClassName: traefik`. Annotations: `cert-manager.io/cluster-issuer: letsencrypt-prod`, `traefik.ingress.kubernetes.io/router.entrypoints: web,websecure`, `traefik.ingress.kubernetes.io/router.middlewares: default-redirect-https@kubernetescrd`. TLS `secretName: {{instance}}-tls`.
- Resources: web/worker each 500m/2 cpu, 1Gi/2Gi mem. Snuba and Kafka 500m/2 cpu, 1Gi/3Gi mem. ClickHouse 1/4 cpu, 2Gi/4Gi mem. Postgres 500m/2 cpu, 1Gi/2Gi mem.
- Labels: `app.kubernetes.io/name: sentry`, `app.kubernetes.io/instance: {{instance}}`, `app.kubernetes.io/component: <web|worker|cron|ingest|snuba-api|snuba-consumer|relay|postgres|clickhouse|redis|kafka|zookeeper|memcached>`.
- `imagePullSecrets: [{name: ghrc}]` on every pod spec.
- A `sentry-upgrade` Job (Helm-chart-style) that runs `sentry upgrade --noinput` once before web/worker come up — gate it via initContainers or a top-level Job with appropriate ordering.
- Readiness: web `httpGet /_health/ port=9000`, snuba-api `httpGet /health port=1218`, postgres `pg_isready`, clickhouse `httpGet /ping port=8123`, redis `redis-cli PING`.
